creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Using the return value of a void function (no return statement) in any expression context causes a Go panic with "Bug! Unhandled Rad type in Type: 'int'". Affected contexts: arithmetic, comparisons, string interpolation, switch expressions.

**Repro:**
```
fn do_nothing():
    pass

do_nothing() + 1           // Go panic
do_nothing() == null        // Go panic
"result: {do_nothing()}"   // Go panic
switch do_nothing():        // Go panic
```

Should produce a clean error like "Cannot use void value in expression".

**Test files:** `bugs/functions/t20d_void_in_expression.rad`, `t78_fn_void_compare.rad`, `t91_fn_void_interpolation.rad`, `t118_fn_void_in_switch.rad`
title: Void value in expressions causes Go panic
